source_title: π₀.₇: a Steerable Generalist Robotic Foundation Model with Emergent Capabilities

ai_summary.one_liner: Steerable 5B VLA robot foundation model that uses rich context conditioning to combine mixed-quality robot, human, and web data into broad out-of-the-box generalization
ai_summary.problem: Prior VLAs improve with scale but still struggle to recombine skills, follow complex instructions, transfer across embodiments, and use mixed-quality or suboptimal data without averaging incompatible behaviors.
ai_summary.solution: Train a 5B flow-based VLA with diverse prompt context: language/subtask instructions, subgoal images, episode-quality metadata, control-mode labels, and dropout so the model can be steered at test time.
ai_summary.key_result: Seen tasks often exceed 90% success, unseen tasks or unseen task-robot combinations land around 60-80%; the model matches or exceeds specialized π0.6/π0.6* policies on several dexterous tasks and reaches 80% success in UR5e shirt folding, close to expert teleoperators at 80.6%.
limitations: Zero-shot generalization remains lower than in-distribution performance.
It is hard to prove which tasks are truly unseen in such a large heterogeneous dataset.
The training data, annotations, and large-scale evaluation infrastructure are not reproducible from the paper alone.

research_gaps.question:
seen task는 90%를 넘는 경우가 많지만 unseen task나 unseen task-robot 조합은 60-80% 수준으로 낮아진다. zero-shot generalization은 아직 in-distribution 성능과 차이가 크다.
- **저자 언급**: 대규모 heterogeneous dataset에서는 무엇이 진짜 unseen인지 판정하기 어렵다. 논문의 generalization은 완전히 새로운 skill 생성이라기보다, 넓은 데이터에서 배운 behavior의 remix일 수 있다.
**재현성 한계**: 모델 크기, 데이터 규모, annotation pipeline, 로봇 fleet, evaluation infrastructure가 모두 크고 비공개 요소가 많아 외부 연구자가 결과를 재현하거나 공정 비교하기 어렵다.
